text.title: Solution Limitation Assessment
text.summary: Limitation identification, root-cause requirements (5-whys minimum), severity derivation formula, and remediation option constraints.

rule.statement: Extract limitations from defect logs, support tickets, and at least one user-research source; never from team memory alone.
rule.rationale: Tickets-only over-weights the loudest 5 customers; user-research corroboration identifies silent majority pain points that tickets never surface.

list.item: Functional gaps: features that do not work as specified or are missing.
Performance issues: speed, reliability, scalability below threshold.
Usability problems: users cannot complete tasks without workarounds.
Security: known vulnerabilities or compliance violations.
Data: quality, integrity, or access issues affecting business decisions.

rule.statement: Require a minimum 3-entry 5-whys chain for every limitation row; reject any row where the root_cause restates the symptom description.
rule.rationale: Remediation that lands on symptoms produces the same defect next quarter; only root-cause-anchored remediation is durable.

example.note: Limitation: "Export fails for large datasets." Root cause: "Export fails for large datasets." — This is symptom restatement, not root cause. Reject this row and re-run with 5-whys drill.

5-whys chain: (1) Export fails → (2) timeout at 30s → (3) synchronous processing → (4) no async job queue → (5) batch design was never planned for >10k rows. Root cause category: technical. Remediation: implement async export job.

rule.statement: Derive severity from a numeric formula (users_affected x frequency x business_impact); never assert severity without showing the math.
rule.rationale: Without a numeric formula, every limitation is tagged "High" within a week of the register opening — prioritization breaks entirely.

list.item: Critical: affects >50% of users, occurs daily, blocks core business process.
High: affects >20% of users, occurs weekly, creates significant workaround burden.
Medium: affects <20% of users, occurs monthly, has viable workaround.
Low: affects <5% of users, rare, cosmetic or minor inconvenience.

section.title: Remediation Options

rule.statement: Document "Accept" remediation only when every cheaper option (fix, workaround, defer) is explicitly listed and rejected with rationale.
rule.rationale: Accepting without exhausting alternatives is not a decision — it is inaction dressed up as analysis.

section.reference: Limitation register with root cause, severity formula, and remediation columns.
